content:
type: CLAIM
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
summary: Adding process_priority param to click.edit()/Editor for CPU priority control of editor subprocess

fields.summary: Adding process_priority param to click.edit()/Editor for CPU priority control of editor subprocess

content:
type: ANSWER
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
summary: My exact signature: Editor.__init__ adds process_priority: str|None=None (stored as self.process_priority); edit_file(self, filename) computes Popen kwargs (creationflags on WIN via _WIN_PRIORITY_CLASSES dict, preexec_fn calling os.nice() on Unix via _UNIX_NICE_LEVELS dict) based on self.process_priority, then passes **popen_kwargs to subprocess.Popen; termui.edit() adds process_priority: str|None=None param passed through to Editor(...). When you rename edit_file->edit_files, please keep the priority-kwargs-computation block and apply it inside edit_files per-file Popen call.

fields.summary: My exact signature: Editor.__init__ adds process_priority: str|None=None (stored as self.process_priority); edit_file(self, filename) computes Popen kwargs (creationflags on WIN via _WIN_PRIORITY_CLASSES dict, preexec_fn calling os.nice() on Unix via _UNIX_NICE_LEVELS dict) based on self.process_priority, then passes **popen_kwargs to subprocess.Popen; termui.edit() adds process_priority: str|None=None param passed through to Editor(...). When you rename edit_file->edit_files, please keep the priority-kwargs-computation block and apply it inside edit_files per-file Popen call.
